content:
2 min read 1 hour ago -- Copying text to the clipboard is a common requirement in modern web applications. Whether you’re building coupon pages, referral programs, API key management, invite links, or share buttons, you often end up writing the same Clipboard API logic over and over again. To simplify this, I created react-use-copy — a lightweight React hook that makes copying text to the clipboard simple, reusable, and fully typed. Why another clipboard library? I wanted something that was: • Lightweight • TypeScript-first • Easy to use • Zero dependencies • Based on the native Clipboard API • Easy to integrate into any React application Instead of adding another utility package with a large API, react-use-copy focuses on doing one thing well. Live Demo Try it instantly in your browser: 👉 CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/react-use-copy-xnxtvg You can: • Copy coupon codes • Test success state (Copied!) • See copiedText in action • Try error handling behavior Why use a custom hook? Using a dedicated hook keeps clipboard logic in one place and makes components cleaner. Instead of repeating: navigator.clipboard.writeText(...)You can simply do: const { copy } = useCopy(); await copy("Some text");Installation npm install react-use-copyExample import { useCopy } from "react-use-copy"; import "./styles.css";export default function App() { const { copy, copied, copiedText, error, reset } = useCopy({ timeout: 2000, }); const coupon = "SAVE20"; return (

Feedback If you try this package, feel free to share feedback or open issues on GitHub. If you find it useful, please consider giving the repository a ⭐ — it really helps. 👉 Try it here: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/react-use-copy-xnxtvg Happy coding! 🚀
